metadata.title: Exploring Career Paths with Career Cards: A Career Guide for School Students
metadata.publisher: National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)
metadata.development_institute: PSS Central Institute of Vocational Education (PSSCIVE), Bhopal
metadata.target_audience: Secondary and Higher Secondary School Students (Grades 9-12)
metadata.purpose: Career guidance and counselling for school students. Each entry covers: description, personality traits, scholarships, loans, course fees, educational pathway, expected salary, career growth path, work environment, recommended institutes (govt & private), and real-world examples.

careers.title: Aeronautical Engineer
careers.description: An aeronautical engineer is responsible for designing, developing and constructing safe and fuel-efficient machines, which can fly in air and space such as aircraft, helicopters, satellites, missiles, drones and spacecraft. He/she is also responsible for regularly inspecting and issuing clearance of the aircraft for air-worthiness.
personality_traits: You are interested in aviation and aerospace
You are detail oriented and precise
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 in Science (Physics, Chemistry,
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Physics, Chemistry,

careers.career_growth_path: Graduate Engineer Trainee → Junior Engineer → Aeronautical Engineer → Senior Engineer → Principal Engineer → Chief Engineer

work_environment.places_of_work: Indian Air Force, Defence Ministry, Tp Airlines industry, Aviation companies or Research
work_environment.work_environment: It is a full-time job, work hours may —_ Engineer — Principal Engineer extend to more than 8 hours a day. You can either work in offices or at units where equipment is designed, manufactured, assembled or tested.

careers.example_from_field: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, the 11th President of India, began his career as an aeronautical engineer at DRDO and ISRO. He was the chief architect of India's missile development programme, earning him the title 'Missile Man of India'.

careers.title: Automobile Engineer
careers.description: Automobile Engineering deals with the designing, developing, manufacturing, testing, repairing and servicing automobiles such as cars, trucks, motorcycles, scooters etc and the related sub engineering systems. Automobile Engineering uses the features of different elements of engineering such as mechanical, electrical, electronic, software and safety engineering. It forms a subset of the larger automotive engineering.
personality_traits: You may occasionally require to work extended hours to Product Engineering Manager

careers.title: Bioinformatics Engineering
careers.description: Bioinformatics combines Computer Science, Big Data and Life Science to provide solutions to experts working with huge volumes of genetic information. It focuses on molecules like DNA. These Engineers have an expertise in Genetics, Laboratory techniques, and Research protocols, and use Bioinformatics methodology to analyse Biological data.
personality_traits: You are passionate about biology and computing

careers.title: Biomedical Engineer
careers.description: A Biomedical Engineer is a specialist who innovates and designs techonological solutions for the medical industry. They are responsible for designing and creating artificial organs, devices, software and other equipments used in healthcare. They work to improve the quality and effectiveness of patient care, and improve the quality of life as well as the functioning of the medical industry.
personality_traits: You will usually work as a part of a team and expect to
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 in the Science stream.
careers.educational_pathway: 1. Complete 10+2 in the Science stream.

work_environment.places_of_work: Hospitals, Clinics, Research facilities, Medical manufacturing units.
work_environment.work_environment: You can expect to work for minimum 8 hours per day, but in many cases you have to work longer. You will usually work as a part of a team and expect to cooperate and communicate with specialists in other fields.

careers.title: Broadcast Engineer
careers.description: A Broadcast Engineer is essentially in charge of setting up, supervising, maintaining and repairing all the technical, sound, video and audio equipment in television, radio and studio broadcasts. Broadcast media technology includes different techniques of communication that involve sending data over large distances using electronic and electromagnetic methods.
personality_traits: You have keen interest in art and drawing
You have an eye for details
You are good at working independently

careers.title: Ceramic Technologist/Engineer
careers.description: Ceramic technology is the Science of creating objects from inorganic, non-metallic material. A ceramic technologist therefore is a person who makes use of this technology in the field of medicine, construction, electronics, military, optical fibres, sports and transportation and so on.
personality_traits: You are likely to work 5 to 6 days a week and 8 to 9 hours
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 in Science stream (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics).
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10 + 2 in the Science stream (Physics,
Chemistry and Maths).
2. Complete a Bachelor’s degree (B.Sc/BE/B. Tech)
in Ceramic Engineering
Complete a Diploma in Ceramic Engineering
Complete a Bachelor's degree followed by a
Master's degree in the same or allied field.
For admissions, you must qualify entrance
examination conducted at national level (JEE Main,
JEE Advanced etc.) or state level (WBJEE, AP
EAMCET etc.) or institute level (VET, IP etc.).
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment

work_environment.places_of_work: Ceramic factories, government and private research laboratories and consulting firms among others.
work_environment.work_environment: You are likely to work in a studio set up. Local travel is not a part of the job profile. You are likely to work 5 to 6 days a week and 8 to 9 hours every day. This may vary from company to company. Shift system might be available.

careers.example_from_field: Sonali Banerjee is India’s first woman maritime engineer. She pursued a B.E. in Marine Engineering at Kolkata’s Taratala-based Marine Engineering Research Institute (MERI). She was later selected by Mobil Shipping Co. for a gruelling six-month pre-sea course. This hands-on training took her to ports in Singapore, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Hong Kong, Fiji and Australia. On August 26, 2001, Sonali made history when she boarded a Mobil Shipping Co. vessel and officially became the first Indian woman to take charge of a ship's machine room.”
careers.source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 2, April 2025

careers.title: Coast Guard

The ICG is responsible for protecting the maritime and other national interests of India in the maritime zones. They provide protection to offshore and other structures. They also provide protection to fishermen and assist in anti smuggling operations.

careers.title: Indian Administrative Service
careers.description: Officers of the Indian Administrative Service (IAS) hold some of the key positions in the Union government, state governments, public-sector companies and other statutory regulatory institutions such as the Central Bank (RBI), SEBI, and Election Commission. They are primarily responsible for regulating and monitoring the general administration and law and order in the areas allotted to them. This function is then diversified into numerous other functions such as supervising the implementation of developmental schemes and expenditure of public funds.

careers.example_from_field: Gunjan Saxena (born 1975)is an Indian Air Force officer and former helicopter pilot. She joined the IAF in 1996 and is a 1999 Kargil War veteran.She is one of the first women to fly in a combat zone. One of her main roles during the Kargil War was to evacuate the wounded from Kargll, transport supplies, and assist in surveillance.She would go on to be part of operations to evacuate over 900 troops, both injured and deceased, from Kargil. In 2004, after serving as a pilot for eight years, her career as a helicopter pilot ended; permanent commissions for women were not available during her time.
careers.source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 2, April 2025

careers.example_from_field: Captain Divya Ajith Kumar is an Army Air Defence officer in the Indian Armed Forces. She is a first-generation soldier who completed her training from Officers Training Academy, Chennai. She is the first woman officer to be conferred with the Sword of Honour, usually given to the best cadet by the Army. She also led the first all-women contingent of the Army on Republic Day in 2015. Kumar currently serves in the Officers Training Academy, Chennai.”
careers.source: NCERT/UNICEF Career Guide for School Students, Volume 2, April 2025

careers.title: Indian Forest Service
careers.description: An Indian Forest Service (IFS) Officer is a key civil servant who works in the Indian Forest Service, one of the three All India Services under the Government of India. IFS officers are responsible for the conservation, management, and protection of the country's forests and wildlife. They work in forest departments at the central, state, and district levels and play a vital role in ensuring the sustainable management of India’s vast natural resources.

careers.title: Indian Home Guard
careers.description: An auxiliary to the Indian Police, the Indian Home Guard is a body which is responsible for enforcing and maintaining order within the community in any kind of emergency. The main duties of a home guard is to help in maintenance of essential services, promote communal harmony, assist the administration in protecting weaker sections, participate in socio-economic and welfare activities and perform Civil Defence duties.
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Minimum Class 10 pass. 10+2 preferred.

may pursue short-term courses in disaster
management, fire safety, first aid, or civil defense to
build foundational knowledge and practical skills for
joining the Home Guard.

Fire Safety can be helpful to understand the basics of
emergency response, public safety, and community
service, which are relevant for Home Guard duties.
Although not mandatory, a Bachelor's degree in any
discipline from a government-recognized university
can be an advantage. Degrees in Physical Education,
Social Work, or Social Sciences may provide relevant
knowledge in community service, law enforcement,
and disaster management.

Management, Public Safety, or Community Service
can deepen your understanding of emergency
response and safety protocols, which may

An Indian Naval officer is expected to deter any and all marine threats to country and to aid the other two armed forces (army and air force) in neutralising any threat to the country. The job of an Indian Naval Officer also includes navigation, rescuing stranded castaways, and general maintenance and upkeep of heavy machinery.

careers.title: Cyber Security Specialist
careers.description: A Cybersecurity Specialist is a professional responsible for protecting an organization’s computer systems, networks, and data from cyber threats. Their primary role is to identify vulnerabilities, prevent cyberattacks, and respond to security breaches. They implement security protocols, conduct risk assessments, and ensure compliance with regulations to safeguard information and maintain the integrity and confidentiality of digital assets.

B.Sc.) in Computer Science and Engineering
After completing a Bachelor's degree you may

careers.career_growth_path: Junior Security Analyst → Security Analyst → Cyber Security Specialist → Security Architect → CISO

work_environment.places_of_work: Different industries such as Banks, Government, Retail, Healthcare and BFSI sectors actively recruit Cyber Security professionals.
work_environment.work_environment: Most Cyber Security Professionals spend roughly 40 hours a week in the office for full-time employment. However, during technology releases or program updates there are often longer hours required. Sometimes systems need updates or maintenance overnight and over the weekends. Cybersecurity professionals may also travel by providing consultation at a variety of client locations.

careers.title: Commercial Driver
careers.description: A Commercial Driver is trained and certified to drive a commercial vehicle professionally. They possess commercial driver's licence and operate large and heavy vehicles, including trucks, buses, and trailers.
personality_traits: You pay attention to details while working
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Minimum Class 8 pass. Driving licence required.
careers.educational_pathway:
Certificate
Eligibility: Basic literacy and knowledge of traffic rules and
safety are helpful.
Duration: 3 to 6 months.
Examples: Certificate in Commercial Vehicle Driving,
Certificate in Heavy Vehicle Operation.
Diploma
Eligibility: High school diploma or equivalent.
Duration: 1 to 2 years.
Examples: Diploma in Commercial Vehicle Driving, Diploma
in Road Safety and Transport Management.
Undergraduate
Eligibility: High school diploma or equivalent.
Duration: 3 to 4 years.
Examples: Bachelor’s in Logistics and Supply Chain
Management, Bachelor’s in Transport Management.
Postgraduate
Eligibility: Bachelor’s degree in a related field.
Duration: 1 to 2 years.
Examples: Master’s in Transport and Logistics Management,
Master’s in Supply Chain Management.
Doctoral
Eligibility: Master's degree in a related field.
Duration: 3 to 5 years.
Examples: PhD in Transportation Engineering, PhD in
Logisti
careers.career_growth_path: Helper → Commercial Driver → Senior Driver → Transport Supervisor → Fleet Manager

work_environment.places_of_work: Logistics Companies or any company that needs to get either raw material or Assistant Driver/Helper —- Commercial finished products from one point to another. Vehicle Driver Level 3 — Commercial Vehicle Driver Level 4 — Commercial
work_environment.work_environment: You will spend a lot of time driving the vehicle. There are no defined work hours, but you are likely to spend 40 or more hours a week on the road to ensure timely delivery. You must know the fundamentals of the mechanical aspects of the vehicle. This is because some of the maintenance jobs have to be completed by you.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: You can own a truck and take up Vehicle Driver Trainer contractual work or start your own company.

careers.title: Criminologist
careers.description: Criminology is the study of crime and deviant behaviour. It deals with understanding the root cause of the crime and its effective prevention. A Criminologist studies crime and society's response to crime. They conduct research and investigations, collect and analyze qualitative and quantitative data surrounding crime, ultimately providing insights and solutions to prevent crime in a community. Criminologists work closely with the police and policymakers to offer suggestions on policing strategies and proactive policing.

The role of an economist includes giving economical and financial advice to businesses, companies, brands or even the government. They are also responsible for evaluating the company’s business stance and give strategy suggestions keeping in mind economic outlook. They guide governments and organisations in making financial decisions in order to improve the country's economy.

careers.title: Geographer
careers.description: Geographer studies the Earth and its land, features, its inhabitants and how they interact with their surrounding. With extensive knowledge of natural phenomena, they identify patterns and make predictions about how different natural events and human behaviors can impact ecosystems, including the atmosphere surrounding Earth, bodies of water and the habitats of animals and plants. The two main types of geographers are human geographers and physical geographers. Geographers use several technologies in their work, such as GIS, remote sensing, and global positioning systems (GPS).
personality_traits: You are interested in the Earth and its environments
You enjoy using maps and GIS
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 in any stream. Arts or Science preferred.
careers.educational_pathway:

Geography as a subject.
2. Complete Bachelor’s degree in Geography
/Earth Sciences (B.A./B.Sc.) followed by
Master's degree in Cartography/Urban and Town
Planning/Regional Planning/Physiography/
Climatology.
Complete Bachelor's degree in Geography/
Earth Sciences (B.A./B.Sc.) followed by
Master's degree in Cartography/Urban and Town
Planning/Regional Planning/Physiography/
Climatology and then complete PhD in a relevant
subject.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment

work_environment.places_of_work: State/Local Government, Architectural and Engineering Firms, Schools, Colleges, Research Institutions.
work_environment.work_environment: You may have to travel to do fieldwork. You may travel to foreign countries or remote locations to gather data and observe geographic features, such as the landscape and environment.

careers.title: Historian
careers.description: A Historian studies past events using a wide range of sources such as written records, physical artifacts, photographs, interviews, films, letters and other primary source documents. They collect information from these sources to answer questions about what was happening in the past and why at a given time. They try to understand how and why things change over time. History can be divided into three major specialisations — archaeology, museology and archival studies.
personality_traits: You are analytical and detail oriented
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Complete 10+2 in Arts stream with History.
careers.educational_pathway:
Certificate
Eligibility: Class 12 from a recognized board.
Duration: 6 months to 1 year.
Examples: Advanced Certificate in Ancient Indian
History, Certificate in Historical Studies.
Diploma
Eligibility: Class 12 with a min. of 50% aggregate
Duration: 1 to 2 years.
Examples: Diploma in Archaeology, Diploma _ in
Museology.
Undergraduate
Eligibility: Class 12 with a min. of 50% aggregate
Duration: 3 years.
Examples: B.A. in History, B.A. in Ancient History.
Postgraduate
Eligibility: Bachelor's degree in History or a related
field with at least 50% marks.
Duration: 2 years.
Examples: M.A. in History, M.A. in Ancient Indian
History and Archaeology.
Doctoral
Eligibility: Master's degree in History or a related
discipline with a minimum of 55% marks.
Duration: 3 to 5 years.
Examples: Ph.D. in History, Ph.D. in Archaeology
Short-Term Courses: Certificate in Archival Studies.

work_environment.places_of_work: Research Firms, Museums, Universities, Public Sector Firms or Historical Societies, Media, Publishing Industry.
work_environment.work_environment: You are likely to spend most of your time in offices, classrooms, and libraries. Working hours are flexible. You may have to travel to distant cities or countries. You are expected to visit archives or historic sites for research.
